pi_items.source: Task file path specification; verified as consistent with Android device mount configuration in env.json
Confirmed as a first-generation EGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitor used for non-small cell lung cancer treatment; validated via PMC publications on EGFR inhibitors
Confirmed as a second-generation irreversible EGFR inhibitor used for NSCLC; validated via Drugs.com and PMC EGFR inhibitor databases
Confirmed as a third-generation mutant-specific EGFR inhibitor (T790M) for NSCLC; validated via multiple PMC publications and clinical resources
Confirmed as Class III antiarrhythmic drug (FDA-approved for life-threatening ventricular arrhythmias); validated via NCBI StatPearls and Mayo Clinic

Evidence document (results_key.png) from Liverpool Cancer iChart app shows five colors: Red (contraindicated), Orange (potential interaction requiring monitoring), Yellow (weak interaction), Green (no significant interaction), Grey (insufficient data). Metadata includes both 'grey' and 'gray' which are spelling variants of the same color.

Case report in Anti-Cancer Drugs journal and PubMed (PMID: 29420339) documents probable drug-drug interaction causing severe neurotoxicity; mechanism: amiodarone inhibits P-glycoprotein efflux pump, increasing erlotinib blood-brain barrier penetration
Afatinib is a P-glycoprotein substrate; amiodarone is a P-gp inhibitor (per Drugs.com interaction checker); coadministration increases afatinib exposure; dose reduction may be required

Cancer-druginteractions.org and DrugBank document QT/QTc prolongation risk; concomitant use increases risk of torsade de pointes; amiodarone also poses cardiac safety concerns when combined with osimertinib

pi_items.metadata_value: Implied by env.json and task description
pi_items.verified_value: Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app confirmed to exist
pi_items.source: App listed on Google Play and Apple App Store (com.liverpooluni.ichartoncology); developed by Radboud UMC, University of Liverpool, and Clubzap Ltd; offline application with traffic light system for cancer drug interactions
Cancer-druginteractions.org archive notice and Liverpool University documentation state the interaction checker content was last updated in June 2022

privileged_info_summary: The task requires an agent to search the Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app for drug-drug interaction information. Three EGFR inhibitors (Erlotinib, Afatinib, and Osimertinib) are real cancer medications; Amiodarone is a real antiarrhythmic drug. All three EGFR inhibitors have documented serious interactions with Amiodarone: Erlotinib and Afatinib both involve P-glycoprotein inhibition (increasing drug exposure); Osimertinib interaction involves QT prolongation risk. The Liverpool app uses a verified traffic light color system: Red (contraindicated), Orange (potential interaction requiring monitoring), Yellow (weak interaction), Green (no significant interaction), and Grey (insufficient data). The app database content was last updated June 2022. The app exists and is available on mobile platforms.
